input.background: An important historical case-control completed by Doll and Hill found that 647 of the 649 lung cancer cases were smokers. In contrast, 622 of 649 controls smoked.
input.question: Display these data as a 2-by-2 table.

output.answer:
\begin{tabular}{r|r|r|r}
\hline Smoke & Cases & Non-cases & Total \\ 
\cline { 1 - 4 }+ & 647 & 622 & 1269 \\ 
\cline { 1 - 4 } Total & 22 & 27 & 29 \\ 
\cline { 1 - 4 } Total & 649 & 649 & 1298 \\ 
\hline
\end{tabular}

LungCancerCases.description: Number of lung cancer cases who were smokers and non-smokers.

ControlCases.description: Number of control cases who were smokers and non-smokers.
